LAW AND MOTION 302, NO APPEARANCES ON THIS MATTER. THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS: DEFENDANT PLANT INSULATION COMPANY'S AMENDED MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT IS GRANTED. DEFENDANT SHIFTED THE BURDEN OF PRODUCING EVIDENCE BASED ON PLAINTIFF'S FACTUALLY DEVOID DISCOVERY. PLAINTIFF FAILS TO PRESENT ADMISSIBLE EVIDENCE THAT PLAINTIFF WAS EXPOSED TO ASBESTOS AS A CONSEQUENCE OF DEFENDANT'S ACTS. THE COURT STRIKES THE COHEN DECLARATION FOR LACK OF FOUNDATION DEMONSTRATING THAT THE RE-ENTRAINMENT THEORY IS GENERALLY ACCEPTED IN THE SCIENTIFIC COMMUNITY. EVEN IF ADMITTED, PLAINTIFF'S EVIDENCE IS SPECULATIVE THAT ANY OF THE WORK DONE BY DEFENDANT CAUSED HARM TO PLAINTIFF. PREVAILING PARTY TO PREPARE A FORM OF ORDER. JUDGE: PATRICK J. MAHONEY, REPORTER: MARIA TORREANO, CSR 8600

AMENDED MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
Off Calendar Oct-30-2008 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: No appearances on this matter. The Court adopted its tentative ruling as follows: DEFENDANT PLANT INSULATION COMPANY'S AMENDED MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT is granted. Defendant shifted the burden of producing evidence based on plaintiff's factually devoid discovery. Plaintiff fails to present admissible evidence that plaintiff was exposed to asbestos as a consequence of defendant's acts. The Court strikes the Cohen Declaration for lack of foundation demonstrating that the re-entrainment theory is generally accepted in the scientific community. Even if admitted, plaintiff's evidence is speculative that any of the work done by defendant caused harm to plaintiff. Prevailing party to prepare a form of order. (302/PJM/VC) Judge: Patrick J. Mahoney, Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR 8600
